Name: React Hooks
Description: Modern React state management using functional components
Best_Practice: Use functional components with hooks instead of class components
Anti_Pattern: Don't use class components for new code unless absolutely necessary

Name: TypeScript Interfaces
Description: Define object shapes and contracts with interfaces
Best_Practice: Define clear interfaces for all public APIs and data structures
Anti_Pattern: Don't use 'any' type everywhere as it defeats the purpose of TypeScript
Example: interface User { id: number; name: string; }

Name: Async/Await
Description: Handle asynchronous operations with async/await syntax
Best_Practice: Use async/await for cleaner asynchronous code instead of .then() chains
Anti_Pattern: Don't mix async/await with .then() in the same function

Name: React Keys
Description: Use unique keys when rendering lists in React
Best_Practice: Always provide stable unique keys when mapping arrays to components
Anti_Pattern: Don't use array index as key if items can be reordered or filtered

Name: Error Boundaries
Description: Catch rendering errors in React components
Best_Practice: Wrap major sections of your app with error boundaries to prevent crashes
Anti_Pattern: Don't ignore error handling in components

Name: Semantic HTML
Description: Use semantic HTML elements for better accessibility
Best_Practice: Use semantic elements like nav main article section instead of divs
Anti_Pattern: Don't use div for everything when semantic elements are available

Name: CSS Variables
Description: Use CSS custom properties for consistent theming
Best_Practice: Define CSS variables in :root for colors spacing and common values
Anti_Pattern: Don't hardcode color values throughout your CSS

Name: Responsive Images
Description: Optimize images for different screen sizes
Best_Practice: Use srcset and lazy loading for images to improve performance
Anti_Pattern: Don't use fixed-size images for all devices

Name: TypeScript Generics
Description: Create reusable type-safe functions and components
Best_Practice: Use generics to make functions and classes work with multiple types
Anti_Pattern: Don't duplicate code for different types when generics can be used

Name: Component Composition
Description: Build complex UIs by composing simple components
Best_Practice: Prefer composition over inheritance for component reusability
Anti_Pattern: Don't create deep inheritance hierarchies for components

Name: Immutable State
Description: Never mutate state directly in React
Best_Practice: Use spread operator or methods that return new objects to update state
Anti_Pattern: Don't directly modify state objects or arrays

Name: API Error Handling
Description: Handle API errors properly with try-catch
Best_Practice: Always wrap API calls in try-catch and handle different error types
Anti_Pattern: Don't assume API calls will always succeed without error handling

Name: Debouncing Input
Description: Debounce expensive operations triggered by user input
Best_Practice: Debounce search inputs and auto-save operations to reduce API calls
Anti_Pattern: Don't trigger API calls on every keystroke without debouncing

Name: CSS Flexbox
Description: Use flexbox for flexible responsive layouts
Best_Practice: Use flexbox for one-dimensional layouts and alignment
Anti_Pattern: Don't use floats for layout when flexbox is more appropriate

Name: Local Storage Safety
Description: Safely use localStorage with error handling
Best_Practice: Always wrap localStorage operations in try-catch for quota errors
Anti_Pattern: Don't assume localStorage is always available or has unlimited space
